The other guy who was not as good a copywriter, but it was brilliant strategic, looks at the same argument and says, well, I think he missed a, what I call it, making the money connection, a bridge. So he sits down and this is, many years ago, he created a, a concept called Van Plyst and Tissany. Do you remember that? Mm-hmm. All right. I, I, I know Chase's stuff. Yeah. And, and, Van Plyst and Tissany. Which was a knock on Van Plyst and our Pell Antibody. And he created the Van Plyst Diamond. And he wrote his ad. And his ad was very well written, but nowhere as well as, as the better co, writer, but who was the tactical guy. So he spent, let's call it the same 30, but instead of getting 42, he gets 27, which doesn't even recoup his, his, his product cost. He instead, now the first one that Tatibai just sent his thing out in a crappy little box and that's it. The, the strategic guy sends his out in a beautiful, rich, velvety bag along with a couple of letters. The first letter is a letter from him thanking profoundly and profusely the recipient for having trust, telling them what they're about to see is going to be, probably exceed their expectation in brilliant, fiery magnificence. However, for warning them when they take it out, they might be a little shocked because it's probably going to look smaller than they think. And it's not that they've been cheated. It's that in order to get this extraordinary magnificence out of the stone, it's got more density, meaning it's heavier per whatever. And it says, when our clients find that, when our clients see that, they're impacted three ways. One, a little disappointed, two, amazingly impressed. And three, they want to get their hands on very larger stones and then they want to go to their jeweler and get them set. And it's a lot of work. And because we have so many people over the world that we're dealing with, we've taken the liberty of taking some of our best five carat, 10 carat, 20 carat stones and pre-setting them in beautiful 14 and 18 carat settings. And because you have shown good faith, blind faith and trust, we'd like to encourage you to send it back and we'll trade it up and give you double credit. We've already included a prepaid return box and a catalog. And I think he got $25 million in sales on the back end. But that's the difference between being strategic and tactical. And you know the two people work. Yeah, I'll check Gary and Chase. Yeah. But I mean, Gary was all front end. Yes. Even though great ad. Oh, great ad. Better ad. Couldn't make enough money on the front end. Difference between tactics and strategy. And so I mean, I go on and on because I have a lot of, but that's probably enough. Yeah. And we won't spend, you of course know the Chase Bank, Bankrapper's story. Yes, I do. But I didn't when I met him. I'll tell you, when I met him, I was told that I came to work, quit a job, went there. I was told we had a business that was doing X and had some number of tens of thousands of members. And when I got in, he was doing a conference somewhere and I called in the data people and I found out that he over exaggerated by about 9000. Yeah. Yeah. And I was shocked. But then you make the most of it. It's like play the ball where you're like, but yeah, I do know, I know all the stories. I know the bodies are buried. All right. Let's quickly do and then we'll do these hot seats. Very well. Let's, let's, let's get five minutes on host beneficiary or what I call host parasite or joint ventures. I called it, I called it host parasite in the beginning. It became politically and socially incorrect. Now we call it host beneficiary. We call it power partnering. We call it strategic alliances. The key is that rather than going out in the open market and working your ass off to try to build a buyer, you can shortcut it because there's already somebody who's already worked and invested years, time, money, goodwill, infrastructure, intellectual capital, human capital, all kinds of stuff, building goodwill. And you just have to be able to know who they are. It can be somebody who's selling to the same people can be the people that buy right before, people buy right after the people buy at the same time, the people that buy the alternative, give you a good example and I'm not going to embarrass you. But how many people here have bought or subscribed to anything else besides Dan stuff for your business? Raise your hand. We all know they're all whores. They all run off. No, I mean how many people here have ever have ever bought any one thing for weight loss? Anybody? Did you ever buy two? Did you ever buy a supplement or a book or a health club membership? So the point is, and I've been a math, well we've done probably $7 billion for clients and for myself, but that's how I built my whole, I did $100 million in two years with about a $400,000 capitalization that started with $30,000 that I just kept parlaying because I played off of everybody else's, I even capitalized on my competitor.
